meaning: visual flight rules [空]目视飞行规则
examples: For example, all a VFR pilot has to do to enter Class C airspace is establish two-way radio contact with ATC.
No explicit permission from ATC to enter is needed, although the pilot must continue to obey all regulations governing VFR flight.
On a clear day, a pilot in controlled airspace can choose a VFR or IFR flight plan, and the FAA regulations were devised in a way which accommodates both VFR and IFR operations in the same airspace.
The AF100 features switchable FILM CAM and VIDEO CAM modes, with VFR operable in FILM CAM.
"This is an important milestone and fantastic news for VFR pilots, " said Airline Owners and Pilots Association President Phil Boyer.
